name: Farmers State Bank, Allen, Oklahoma
description_short: Community bank in Allen, Oklahoma established in 1922, offering checking, savings, CDs, and commercial/agricultural loans with locally-focused service.
description_long:
Farmers State Bank was originally established in 1922 in Clarita, Oklahoma as a locally owned rural community bank before relocating to Allen in 1927. For nearly a century, it has maintained its status as an independently owned and operated institution serving its community. The bank emphasizes continuity and local commitment, with twelve employees who collectively bring over 200 years of banking experience to their operations.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of deposit and loan products. Deposit accounts include regular checking (minimum $100 to open), NOW accounts ($1,500 minimum), and savings accounts ($100 minimum). Loan products encompass commercial, agricultural, real estate, and consumer loans at competitive rates with flexible payment terms. The bank also provides CDs and online banking services through their website. Additional services include bill pay, ATM/debit cards, direct deposits, notary services, money orders, and cashier's checks.

Farmers State Bank distinguishes itself through its community-focused philosophy and free services that many larger institutions charge for. These include free notary services for customers, free bank reconciliation, free checking for churches, free phone transfers, and free faxes to customers. The bank also provides free cashing of Social Security and government assistance checks. The staff emphasizes personal relationships and customer service, positioning themselves as neighbors and friends rather than a faceless institution.

As a small community bank, Farmers State Bank serves a specific niche of rural Oklahoma customers seeking personalized service and local decision-making. However, customers should note the bank's size means more limited physical locations, potentially higher fees on certain services compared to regional institutions, and less developed digital infrastructure. The website content copyright from 2009-2010 raises questions about how current online services and product offerings remain. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: No service charges on student or senior accounts (age 62+) upon request
Free notary services, bank reconciliation, and phone transfers for customers
Free cashing of Social Security and government assistance checks
Bill pay available at $3.95/month for up to 10 payments
12 employees with combined 200+ years banking experience
Nearly 100-year history as independently owned community bank
Competitive interest rates on commercial, agricultural, real estate, and consumer loans
cons: Website appears outdated (copyright 2009-2010), suggesting limited digital modernization
Graduated service charges on checking accounts ($5-7/month) for balances under $700
ATM fees of $0.50 per card annually plus $1.00 per transaction
Dormant account fee of $10/month after 24 months of no activity
Insufficient funds charges at $20/item, with higher charges ($25) on bill pay items
best_for: Rural Oklahoma residents seeking personalized, relationship-based banking
Agricultural and commercial borrowers in the Allen/Clarita area needing local lending decisions
Senior citizens and students eligible for fee waiver checking accounts
Community organizations like churches seeking no-cost banking services
diagnosis: Farmers State Bank is best for rural Oklahoma residents and agricultural/commercial borrowers seeking personalized community banking with local decision-making and free customer services. The primary caveat is that the bank's limited digital presence and outdated website suggest it may not meet the needs of customers seeking modern online banking capabilities or those outside its small geographic service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
